q: There are very few reactions whose enthalpy change can be measured directly by measuring the change in temperature in a calorimeter. In some cases, the reaction is difficult, even impossible, to carry out individually. By using ___, the enthalpy change for such reactions can be determined indirectly.
___ states that the enthalpy change for a chemical reaction is the same, whatever route is taken from reactants to products.
Hess's law can be illustrated by drawing an ___.
Note that the standard enthalpy change for the formation of an element in its ___ is zero and no need to take it into account.
To determine the enthalpy change of reaction from enthalpy changes of formation, the elements are written at the bottom with arrows pointing ___.
To determine the enthalpy change of formation from enthalpy changes of combustion, the corresponding ___ should be considered.
The combustion products (usually ___ and water) are written at the bottom.
To determine the enthalpy change of formation from enthalpy changes of combustion, draw the ___ arrows that go from elements and compounds to combustion products.
Enthalpy changes are due to the breaking and forming of bonds. Breaking a bond ___ energy.
Breaking a bond absorbs energy ($\Delta H$ is ___).
Making a bond ___ energy.
Making a bond releases energy ($\Delta H$ is ___).
The energy needed to make or break bonds is called ___ (bond enthalpy).
The higher the bond enthalpy, the more energy needed to break the bond, and the ___ is the bond.
The symbol for bond enthalpy is ___.
___ refers to the bond enthalpy of a mole of single bonds between carbon and hydrogen atoms.
When new bonds are formed the amount of energy released is the ___ as the amount of energy absorbed when the same type of bond is broken.
___ can also be used to find approximate values of enthalpy change of a reaction using bond enthalpies.
The ___ can be used to calculate the enthalpy change of a reaction that cannot be measured directly.
In burning fuels (such as paraffin, coal, propane, and butane) the ___ reaction takes place during the reaction.
Hand warmers produce heat from the ___ reaction.
The endothermic reaction takes place during cooking food, baking bread, boiling water, and ___ the food.
